17 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • U.S. Forces Kill Al-Qaeda Affiliate Leader Linked to ISIS Ambush on Americans in Syria CENTCOM 17 Jan 2026 -- U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) conducted a strike in northwest Syria on Jan. 16, that resulted in the death of a leader affiliated with Al-Qaeda who had direct ties to an ISIS terrorist responsible for an ambush which killed two U.S. service members and an American interpreter on Dec. 13, 2025.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Joint statement by President Costa and by President von der Leyen on Greenland EC/CEU 17 Jan 2026
  • The Government adopts decision to deploy two officers to Greenland Republic of Slovenia 17 Jan 2026 -- At a correspondence session, the Government of the Republic of Slovenia adopted a decision on the participation of two officers of the Slovenian Armed Forces in the planning and implementation of the international military exercise "Arctic Endurance" led by the Kingdom of Denmark with the aim of strengthening regional security.
  • 'Hands off Greenland': Massive anti-US protests held in Denmark, Greenland Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- Demonstrators have begun taking to the streets in Greenland and Denmark as part of massive rallies in protest of US President Donald Trump and his threats to take over the Arctic island.
  • Trump Puts Tariffs on Eight Countries Over Greenland Sputnik 17 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that the United States will introduce a 10% tariff against eight European countries in February, which will later increase to 25% should a deal for the US to purchase Greenland is not reached.
  • White House Deputy Chief of Staff Calls Denmark 'Tiny Country' Unable to Control Greenland Sputnik 17 Jan 2026 -- White House Deputy Chief of Staff Stephen Miller said on Saturday that Denmark is a "tiny country" that is unable to control Greenland.
  • UN Urges US to Comply With UN Charter Amid Trump's Claims to Greenland - Spokesman Sputnik 17 Jan 2026 -- The United Nations calls on the United States to comply with the Charter amid Washington's plans to "acquire" Greenland and threats of tariffs on countries opposing a takeover, UN Deputy Spokesman Farhan Haq said on Friday.
  • Commander of Denmark's Joint Arctic Command rejects US president's claims on Chinese and Russian ships circling around Greenland Global Times 17 Jan 2026 -- Major General Soren Andersen, Denmark's Joint Arctic Command in Greenland, on January 16 denied US President Donald Trump's claims that Chinese and Russian ships are circling around Greenland and confirmed that he had not seen any Russian or Chinese combat ships in the area in the two and a half years he has been commander, according to media reports.
  • Trump warns of tariffs on nations not backing his Greenland plan People's Daily 17 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Friday that he may impose tariffs on countries that do not support his plan for the United States to "obtain" Greenland.

  • Bipartisan Senate NATO Observer Group Statement on Threat of Tariffs on Allies US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 17 Jan 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH) and Thom Tillis (R-NC), Co-Chairs of the bipartisan Senate NATO Observer Group, issued the following statement regarding the recent threat of tariffs on the United Kingdom, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Finland, Sweden, Denmark and Norway:

  • Ukraine's Ministry of Defence showcased Ukrainian interceptor drones and maritime unmanned systems to the President of Czechia Ukraine MOD 17 Jan 2026
  • Admissions campaign 2026: specializations offered by higher military educational institutions Ukraine MOD 17 Jan 2026

  • China warns of "catastrophic disasters" from unchecked Japanese militarist ambitions People's Daily 17 Jan 2026 -- A Chinese military spokesperson said on Friday that unchecked Japanese militarist ambitions would pose a grave threat to other Asian nations and ultimately bring "catastrophic disasters" upon the Japanese people.

  • ICGS Sankalp's port call at Port Louis, Mauritius as Part of Overseas Deployment in Indian Ocean Region India PIB 17 Jan 2026 -- Indian Coast Guard Ship (ICGS) Sankalp, an Offshore Patrol Vessel, arrived at Port Louis, Mauritius on January 17, 2026 as part of its ongoing Overseas Deployment to Friendly Foreign Countries in the Indian Ocean Region
  • INDIAN NAVY'S FIRST TRAINING SQUADRON ARRIVES AT CHANGI NAVAL BASE, SINGAPORE India PIB 17 Jan 2026 -- Indian Navy's First Training Squadron (1TS), comprising INS Tir, INS Shardul, INS Sujata, and Indian Coast Guard Ship Sarathi, arrived at Changi Naval Base, Singapore, on 15 Jan 2026

  • Iraqi army assumes full control of Ain al-Asad airbase after complete US withdrawal Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- US forces have fully withdrawn from the Ain al-Asad airbase in western Iraq, with the Iraqi army now assuming full control of the strategic facility, Iraqi officials said on Saturday.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Jamaican Prime Minister Holness US Dept. of State 17 Jan 2026

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • Secretary-General on UN at 80: Humanity strongest when we stand as one UN News 17 Jan 2026 -- Powerful forces are lining up to undermine global cooperation, United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres warned at a landmark event in London on Saturday to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the General Assembly, but he urged that "humanity is strongest when we stand as one".
  • Secretary-General's remarks to the United Nations Association of the United Kingdom on the 80th Anniversary of the General Assembly [as delivered] UN Secretary-General 17 Jan 2026
    • Speech for UN-Association 80th anniversary of United Nations General Assembly meeting UK FCDO 17 Jan 2026 -- The Attorney General Lord Hermer KC delivered a speech at the meeting marking the UN-Association's 80th anniversary of the United Nations General Assembly
  • PM meeting with United Nations Secretary General António Guterres: 16 January 2026 Gov.UK 17 Jan 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Prime Minister's Office Statement Israel - Prime Minister 17 Jan 2026 -- "The announcement regarding the composition of the Gaza Executive Board, which is subordinate to the Board of Peace, was not coordinated with Israel and runs contrary to its policy."
  • Israel says Trump's Gaza oversight panel 'contradicts its policy' Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office has criticized the recent formation of a Gaza oversight body announced by the White House, marking a rare public divergence from US President Donald Trump.

  • Hamas says Israeli army, settlers coordinating West Bank attacks Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas says the ongoing attacks by extremist settlers against the Palestinian community in the occupied West Bank are part of a broader and systematic campaign to intimidate residents, complicate their daily lives, and create a new reality that paves the way for annexation and forced displacement of the people.

  • US announces Gaza 'Board of Peace' amid ongoing Israeli ceasefire breaches Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- The administration of US President Donald Trump has announced the executive members of Gaza's so-called Board of Peace, a body purportedly tasked with managing transitional governance in the territory, as Israel continues to violate its ceasefire agreement with Hamas.
  • Trump Invites Turkiye, Argentina, Canada to Join Gaza Peace Council Sputnik 17 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has sent a letter to Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan inviting him to become a member of the Gaza Peace Council, the administration of the Turkish leader said on Saturday.
  • Gaza Peace Board to Include Rubio, Witkoff, Kushner - White House Sputnik 17 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump's Board of Peace for Gaza will include Secretary of State Marco Rubio, US special envoy Steve Witkoff, and Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner, among others, while US Maj. Gen. Jasper Jeffers has been appointed Commander of International Stabilization Force (ISF), the White House said.

  • Gaza humanitarian crisis 'far from being over,' UN aid coordination office warns UN News 17 Jan 2026 -- Three months into the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip, the UN and partners have delivered tonnes of assistance items and carried out critical repairs, but this is only a temporary "Band-Aid" solution, a veteran aid worker has warned.

    • Other Conflicts

  • PH continues lawful operations in WPS, protection of Filipino fishers Philippine News Agency 17 Jan 2026 -- The National Maritime Council (NMC) on Saturday said the Philippines will continue to carry out lawful maritime operations in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) to protect the country's sovereignty and the Filipino fisherfolk.

  • China Expands Its State Media Footprint In Taliban-Run Afghanistan RFE/RL 17 Jan 2026 -- Since the withdrawal of international forces and the Taliban's return to power in August 2021, Afghanistan's once-diverse media landscape has collapsed under a lack of funding and restrictions - and Chinese state media is looking to fill the void.

  • US threatens to reimpose Caesar sanctions on Syria over SDF operations: Report Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- The United States has reportedly threatened to reimpose the so-called Caesar sanctions on Syria over the ruling Arab regime forces' crackdown on the so-called Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF), a Kurdish-led group backed by Washington.

News Reports

  • US judge bars federal agents from pepper spraying, arresting peaceful protesters in Minnesota Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- US immigration agents carrying out a sweeping crackdown against peaceful protests in Minnesota are barred from using pepper spray, nonlethal crowd-control tools or detaining protesters, a federal judge ordered Friday.

  • 115 officials at provincial level or above investigated in China in 2025 People's Daily 17 Jan 2026 -- China filed cases against 115 officials at provincial and ministerial level or above last year, with 69 receiving disciplinary punishment, the country's top anti-graft body said on Saturday.
  • Dialogue, diplomacy essential for durable peace: Chinese envoy Global Times 17 Jan 2026 -- Durable peace is not forged through power projection or unilateral pressure, but through dialogue and diplomacy, said Sun Lei, charge d'affaires of China's Permanent Mission to the United Nations, on Friday.
  • Xi calls for advancing building of China-Canada new strategic partnership Global Times 17 Jan 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney met on Friday for the second time in less than three months, in a bid to chart the course for bilateral ties that began to thaw last year, the Xinhua News Agency reported on Friday.
  • Maiden flight of Chinese privately developed commercial rocket CERES-2 ends in failure Global Times 17 Jan 2026 -- Chinese private space firm Galactic Energy said that its new CERES-2 rocket conducted a maiden flight on Saturday, but an anomaly occurred and the mission ended in failure, according to a statement the company sent to the Global Times on Saturday.

  • PLA drone enters airspace over Taiwan-controlled Dongsha Island CNA 17 Jan 2026 -- A Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) military drone was detected in the airspace of Taiwan-controlled Dongsha Island, internationally known as Pratas Island, at 5:44 a.m. Saturday and departed four minutes later, according to the Ministry of National Defense (MND).
  • The PLA Eastern Theater Command deploys naval and air assets to monitor and track US vessels' Taiwan Straits transits: spokesperson Global Times 17 Jan 2026 -- From January 16 to 17, the US guided-missile destroyer USS John Finn and the oceanographic survey vessel USNS Mary Sears transited the Taiwan Straits. The PLA Eastern Theater Command deployed naval and air assets to monitor and track the US vessels' movements, ensuring effective response and management. The PLA Eastern Theater Command remains on high alert to resolutely safeguard national sovereignty and security, as well as regional peace and stability, said Senior Colonel Xu Chenghua, spokesperson of the PLA Eastern Theater Command, on Saturday.
  • Drone flight training in the airspace near China's Dongsha Island entirely legitimate and lawful: PLA Southern Theater Command spokesperson Global Times 17 Jan 2026 -- The Southern Theater Command has in recent days organized drone flight training in the airspace near China's Dongsha Island, which was entirely legitimate and lawful, said Senior Colonel Tian Junli, spokesperson for the PLA Southern Theater Command, on Saturday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 17 Jan 2026
  • CTi News reporter, 5 military personnel detained over alleged leaks to China CNA 17 Jan 2026 -- A CTi TV reporter and five active-duty and retired service members were detained incommunicado on Saturday for their alleged involvement in a scheme where the journalist bribed military personnel to leak information to "Chinese individuals," according to Ciaotou prosecutors.
  • China opposes countries with diplomatic ties signing deals of sovereign implications or official nature with China's Taiwan region: FM on so-called US-Taiwan 'trade deal' Global Times 17 Jan 2026 -- China always firmly opposes countries having diplomatic relations with China negotiating and signing any agreement of sovereign implication or official nature with China's Taiwan region, a Foreign Ministry spokesperson said on Friday.
  • China firmly opposes any form of official exchanges between its diplomatic partners and Taiwan region: spokesperson People's Daily 17 Jan 2026 -- China firmly opposes any form of official exchanges between countries having diplomatic relations with China and the Taiwan region, foreign ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday.
  • "Taiwan independence" secessionists to be swept into history dustbin: spokesperson People's Daily 17 Jan 2026 -- The "Taiwan independence" separatist forces running counter to people's will are doomed to be thrown into the dustbin of history, a Chinese defense spokesperson said on Friday.

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Speech at Conference in Celebration of 80th Anniversary of Founding of Socialist Patriotic Youth League KCNA 17 Jan 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made a speech at the conference in celebration of the 80th anniversary of the founding of the Socialist Patriotic Youth League.

  • Ayatollah Khamenei: US president main 'culprit' in killings, destruction during recent riots in Iran Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei says Iran considers US President Donald Trump the main culprit for the killings and destruction in recent riots.
  • Iran dismantles terrorist cell behind armed attack in Tehran Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Ministry of Intelligence has successfully dismantled a terrorist team that had infiltrated the country through the western borders to cause casualties during recent riots and had carried out a criminal armed attack in Tehran.
  • Iran restores SMS access, eases curbs on domestic messengers Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- Iran has decided to gradually ease restrictions on public access to messaging services on mobile phones, including Short Message Service (SMS), following riots and terrorist acts that affected the country earlier this month.
  • Trump Says He Convinced Himself Not To Strike Iran RFE/RL 17 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump says he decided on his own not to launch military strikes against Iran, crediting Tehran's alleged cancellation of hundreds of scheduled executions as the decisive factor in his decision to hold back from military action.
  • Top general warns enemies waging media war against Iran after riots Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- The Chief of Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces, Major General Abdolrahim Mousavi, says the Islamic Republic's adversaries try to distort realities through their media empire and cognitive warfare.
  • Trump, Netanyahu orchestrated deadly riots to destabilize Iran: Hezbollah chief Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- The secretary general of Lebanon's Hezbollah resistance movement has hailed Iran as the fortress of resistance and freedom, saying the United States and Israel orchestrated the recent riots to destabilize the country.
  • Iranian Doctors, Witnesses Describe Heavy Casualties After Protests RFE/RL 17 Jan 2026 -- Iranian authorities have plunged the country into what witnesses describe as a near-total digital blackout in response to deadly nationwide protests against the Islamic republic, sharply limiting the flow of information from inside the country.
  • Iran reports record natural gas processing capacity Press TV 17 Jan 2026 -- Iran's state gas company says it has reached a daily record in natural gas processing, amid rising demand from household, power plant, and industrial sectors.
  • UN Security Council holds emergency meeting on Iran People's Daily 17 Jan 2026 -- The United Nations Security Council held an emergency meeting on Thursday regarding the situation in Iran, with many members calling for de-escalation and opposing the use or threat of force against Iran.

  • Press conference - Parliament House, Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 17 Jan 2026

  • Vice Minister Funakoshi's visit to the U.S. Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 17 Jan 2026
